prompt:
Our booking service needs to exchange calendar invites with the outside world, and pulling in a full iCalendar library for the four properties we actually use is overkill. Build me a small ICS codec, ics.ts, exporting formatCalendar(events) and parseCalendar(text). An event is { summary, dtstart, dtend, description? } with the dates kept as opaque strings like '20260712T090000Z' (we don't interpret them here).

On the way out: a single VCALENDAR (VERSION:2.0) wrapping one VEVENT per event, CRLF line endings, properties in SUMMARY / DTSTART / DTEND / DESCRIPTION order, DESCRIPTION only when the event has one. Text values need RFC-style escaping — backslash, semicolon, comma get a backslash, raw newlines become the two characters \n. And the classic ICS gotcha: content lines longer than 75 characters must be folded, continuation lines starting with a single space.

On the way in: be liberal. Accept CRLF or bare LF, unfold continuation lines (space or tab), skip blank lines, ignore properties we don't model (UID, LOCATION, X-anything) and ignore property parameters (SUMMARY;LANGUAGE=en:... still reads as a summary). Unescape text values. But don't silently swallow garbage: missing BEGIN:VCALENDAR, a missing END:VCALENDAR, a VEVENT that's never closed, an event without SUMMARY, or a content line with no colon should each throw an Error whose message actually tells you what's wrong (the no-colon case should quote the offending line).

parse(format(events)) has to be the identity, including for text stuffed with commas, backslashes and newlines, and for lines long enough to fold. Acceptance tests are in test_ics.ts — run `node --test test_ics.ts`, and leave that file alone. No dependencies.
